title: Crepis keniensis
intro: Crepis keniensis, also known as African hawk's beard, is a plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae) that is native to East Africa. Traditionally, Crepis keniensis has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Crepis keniensis include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Crepis keniensis is commonly prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as a powder or capsule supplement, to harness its potential health benefits.
taxonomy: Crepis keniensis is classified as a species within the genus Crepis, which belongs to the family Asteraceae. This classification places it within the group of dandelion-like plants.
